meta_title: For Freelancers: freelance pitching to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to freelance pitching and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelance Pitching: How to Win Clients Without a Portfolio
content.seo_description: Stop worrying about a lack of portfolio. Learn how to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to build high-converting freelance pitches and land your first client.
content.answer_block: You don't need a legacy portfolio to land high-ticket freelance work. Instead, build 'proof-of-capability' assets using AI-driven rapid prototyping. By utilizing Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for implementation, you can ship production-grade micro-tools that serve as live, interactive demonstrations of your engineering expertise to prospective clients.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the freelancer's greatest enemy. You have the skills, the drive, and the technical aptitude, yet every lead hits a wall the moment they ask, 'Can I see your previous work?' Relying on static PDFs or outdated GitHub repos is a losing game in an era where speed is the only currency that matters.

We are shifting the paradigm from 'showing' to 'deploying.' By treating your pitch as a product, you move from a commodity service provider to a high-value technical partner. This guide leverages the raw power of Antigravity and Claude Code to transform your lack of history into an advantage—by shipping lightning-fast, bespoke solutions that prove your competence in real-time.

steps.title: Define the High-Impact Micro-Tool
steps.description: Identify a specific pain point for your target niche. Instead of a generic portfolio, build a tool that solves one expensive problem. This proves immediate ROI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Define a 48-hour micro-SaaS project scope that solves [INSERT NICHE PROBLEM]. Outline the database schema, core API endpoints, and the primary UI component required for a functional MVP.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate project scaffold based on the Antigravity schema provided for [INSERT PROJECT NAME]'

steps.title: Architecting with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate and structure. This ensures your code is professional, modular, and scalable from the first line.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready Next.js project structure with Tailwind CSS, Supabase integration, and error handling. Include a clean directory structure for services, components, and hooks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Implement the directory structure and core configuration files generated by Antigravity'

steps.title: Rapid Logic Implementation via Claude Code
steps.description: Inject the core business logic into your scaffold. Focus on the 'wow' factor—the specific feature that solves the client's problem.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the business logic for [INSERT FEATURE]. Ensure clean separation of concerns and type safety with TypeScript.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Implement the business logic for [INSERT FEATURE] in the services directory'

steps.title: Polishing the User Interface
steps.description: Design is the silent salesman. Use Claude Code to ensure the UI is responsive, modern, and looks like a premium SaaS product.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a high-converting landing page component using Tailwind CSS that highlights the value proposition of this tool.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Apply the landing page UI components and ensure mobile responsiveness'

steps.title: Deployment and Client Demo Prep
steps.description: Deploy your project to a live URL. Your pitch is no longer a conversation; it's a link to a working product.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a checklist for a production-ready deployment, including environment variable management and security best practices.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Verify build integrity and prepare deployment script for Vercel'

pro_tips: Never send a link alone; record a 60-second Loom video walking through the code architecture to show you are a technical lead, not just a prompt-user.
Build 'Ghost Features'—functionality that hints at deeper capabilities, making your micro-tool feel like part of a massive platform.
Use Claude Code to document your build process in a README.md; clients value the ability to read your technical thought process as much as the app itself.
Target 'Micro-Niches' where your specific tool solves a $5k+ problem; the smaller the niche, the higher your perceived value.
Always include a 'Request a Customization' CTA inside your demo app, turning a static portfolio piece into a lead-gen funnel.
content.next_steps: Once your demo is live, draft a cold-outreach script that focuses entirely on the client's problem, using your new tool as the primary proof of your ability to solve it.
content.conversion_block: Stop pitching your past and start building your future. By shipping real software instead of static files, you bypass the competition and position yourself as a top-tier engineer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
